name: Akarshan Arora
title: VLSI Engineer & Developer
heroDescription: I create interactive simulations and visualizations that turn abstract VLSI and software concepts into hands-on learning experiences.

about.headline:
10+ years in VLSI
power integrity
about.description: Senior EMIR CAD Engineer at Microsoft, previously at Qualcomm and NXP. I build automation and AI-assisted debug workflows that cut analysis turnaround from days to hours — so engineers spend time fixing problems, not finding them.

skills.label: VLSI
skills.detail: Power Delivery · EMIR · IR Drop · EM Analysis

projects.description: Things I build on weekends to make VLSI concepts more accessible — interactive, visual, and free for everyone.

items.name: PDN SPR Visualizer
items.description: Analyze shortest path resistance in an interactive 3D power grid. Visualize optimal current paths through multi-layer metal stacks in real-time.

items.name: IR Drop Visualizer
items.description: Explore voltage distribution in an interactive 3D landscape. Watch columns shrink and turn red as current flows, revealing dangerous IR drop zones.

content.description: I share bite-sized technical content on VLSI EMIR concepts, Python automation tips, and CAD workflows on LinkedIn.

items.title: Python for VLSI
items.description: For professionals starting their automation journey — interview prep, CLI tools, common pitfalls, VLSI plots.

items.title: VLSI PDN Guidance
items.description: For people seeking job opportunities in VLSI CAD/PDN — power delivery, EMIR analysis, and tool flows.

items.title: Redhawk SeaScape
items.description: Guidance on how to use the SeaScape platform for VLSI power integrity analysis.

items.title: Career Guidance
items.description: Navigating career paths in VLSI, CAD development, and the semiconductor industry.

testimonials: Superb easy explanation. Good insight provided overall about EMIR learning.
First session was very helpful, with introduction of Python basics. Looking for more sessions on dashboard development.
Very nice session and your way of explanation is pretty good. Looking forward to listening at you again.
Insightful session — he gave me pointers where I should focus, and materials to use. Overall very helpful.
Really helpful as a kickstart to learning Python for VLSI. He also guided to additional resources.
